codeReview.summary: The fix correctly addresses the race condition bug. The original code declared a mutex as a local variable inside each goroutine, providing no synchronization between concurrent exports. The fix moves the mutex to be a field of the Client struct, ensuring all goroutines share the same mutex and properly synchronize writes to the manifest file.

issues.description: Error return from WriteManifest is still ignored with '_'. This was pre-existing and not part of the fix scope, but could be improved to log or handle manifest write failures.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Pattern of using a mutex field on a shared struct for concurrent access to shared resources (like file writes) is a clean solution. The Client struct holds the mutex and all methods that need synchronization use c.manifestMu. This pattern could be useful for other concurrent operations in the codebase.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: pkg/confluence/client.go:23 - manifestMu sync.Mutex field; pkg/confluence/export.go:219-222 - c.manifestMu.Lock()/Unlock() usage

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the TDD procedure correctly as documented in backend-worker skill: wrote failing test first (concurrent write test that would fail without proper mutex), implemented fix, verified with race detection flag.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: (1) Test written first with -race flag design, (2) Fix implemented, (3) 'go test ./pkg/confluence/... -race -v' passed with all 18 tests

summary: PASS - The mutex bug fix is correctly implemented. The race condition was caused by declaring a mutex as a local variable inside each goroutine, which provided no synchronization. The fix properly moves the mutex to be a Client struct field (c.manifestMu), ensuring all concurrent goroutines share the same mutex. A concurrent write test (200 operations) verifies the fix works correctly with race detection enabled. One minor observation: the WriteManifest error return is still ignored, but this is pre-existing and not within the fix scope.
